title: Services, EndpointSlices, DNS, Discovery, and Traffic Policies · Kubernetes Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release review compares fresh services, endpointslices, dns, discovery, and traffic policies choices and accepts only answers tied to observable API, controller, workload, network, storage, or policy evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for service discovery and endpoint evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Pod IP identity, node routing, ports, host network exceptions, replacement, dual-stack boundaries, and application bind behavior.
Trace Service selectors, EndpointSlices, readiness and serving conditions, manual endpoints, topology hints, and stale endpoint diagnosis.
Choose ClusterIP, headless, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName, or selectorless Service from discovery, exposure, platform, and security constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy, keep K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs.

content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy symptom.

At Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs.

For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Local traffic policy always improves availability and balances requests evenly.

stimulus.title: K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Local traffic policy always improves availability and balances requests evenly.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.
